message:
sec(ipc): [HIGH] constant-time token compare in IpcAuth::authorize

## Why

CVSS v3.1 (estimated): AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N ≈ 5.7 Medium
(treated as HIGH under the skill's severity ladder — it is an auth
escalation via a well-known side-channel class).

`IpcAuth::authorize` looked up the presented token with

    self.tokens.iter().find(|candidate| candidate.token == token)

`String::eq` short-circuits on the first byte mismatch. Over a Unix
socket a co-tenant process can measure the elapsed time of
`authorize()` and recover the stored token one byte at a time (the
classic HMAC/secret-compare timing side-channel). `.find()` amplifies
the leak further: once a candidate matches, iteration stops — so an
attacker can also distinguish "token at the head of the vec" from
"token at the tail." Neither leak is acceptable for an auth gate.

The socket is local-only today, but:
- Multi-tenant hosts and container co-tenancy do run multiple ft
  processes under different users against the same parent socket dir.
- IPC tokens gate real capability (process control, storage access,
  pane injection via downstream MCP surfaces).
- Precedent exists in this crate: `distributed::constant_time_eq`
  already uses the exact accumulator-based pattern for the identical
  threat on the distributed cluster auth path.

## Fix

1. Introduce `ipc_constant_time_eq(&str, &str) -> bool` that
   - always scans `max(a.len(), b.len())` bytes,
   - ORs every byte XOR into a single `usize` accumulator,
   - XORs the length difference into the accumulator up-front so
     unequal lengths are detected without early-return.
2. Replace the `.find()` short-circuit with an explicit loop that
   visits every candidate in `self.tokens`, records the first match
   into `matched: Option<&IpcAuthToken>`, and keeps iterating. The
   loop runtime is now O(tokens × max_token_len) regardless of
   where (or whether) a match occurs. The first-match-wins semantics
   are preserved so the subsequent expiry/scope checks are unchanged.

`distributed::constant_time_eq` is kept as-is (private to its module);
the ipc helper is a local copy rather than a cross-module import to
avoid re-exporting the internal distributed auth plumbing.

## Tests (7 new)

Functional contract pins for the helper (can't statistically assert
a timing property in a noisy CI, but can lock the loop shape and
every branch it needs to take):

- `ipc_constant_time_eq_matches_on_equal_strings`
- `ipc_constant_time_eq_rejects_first_byte_mismatch`
- `ipc_constant_time_eq_rejects_last_byte_mismatch` (the key
  regression: ensures we don't return early on first-byte equality)
- `ipc_constant_time_eq_rejects_different_lengths`
- `ipc_constant_time_eq_empty_strings_match`

And for the authorize() loop:

- `ipc_auth_accepts_match_anywhere_in_vec` — token at index 2 of 3
  authorizes identically to one at index 0, proving we no longer
  break on first candidate match.
- `ipc_auth_rejects_prefix_of_valid_token` — prefixes of the real
  token (lengths 0, 1, 11, 12 against a 13-char token) all fail,
  co-locating the functional regression with the fix.

## Not changed

- On-wire envelope format, scope enum, or expiry semantics — pure
  compare-path change.
- `distributed.rs` — already constant-time, untouched.
- No new crate dependency (subtle, constant_time_eq). Local helper
  mirrors the existing pattern to keep the dep graph stable.
- No logging change: InvalidToken messages stay generic (no
  "partial match detected" leak).

`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features`:
compiles clean on /tmp/ft-p6-phase-c in 1m49s. Test-binary
compilation is currently blocked by pre-existing concurrent-agent
errors in diagnostic.rs and workflows/handlers.rs; the new tests
here are pure u8/bool logic and will compile once those are fixed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
